creator: feenberg@nber.org
text:
On Fri, 25 Sep 2009, Naram Qashat wrote:
> OK, so I don't know how, but you have the wrong MD5 checksum for the file

This was a clean install from a CD-ROM purchased from FreeBSD Mall. I 
won't be the only one with this MD5 checksum.

Since sending may message, I decided to give "NO_CHECKSUM=YES" another 
try, and the compilers do install now. So I am all set.

> here.  It should be a50fff6bc1e1542451722e2650cb53b4, and that's the MD5 
> checksum in FreeBSD's ports tree CVS.  I did submit a patch which incorrectly 
> had the above checksum, but that part of the patch was not committed.  Those

Committed or not, it made it on to the "official" CD. A bit surprising the 
portsnap update didn't fix this.

In any case thank you for your help.

Sincerely,

Daniel Feenberg
feenberg@nber.org
